_note: Canonical machine-readable pins for the kit's DEFAULT operator tools (ADR-20260612). Single source of truth read by setup/install-external-tools.{ps1,sh}; the human lifecycle, rationale, and gotchas live in setup/EXTERNAL-TOOLS.md. DEFAULT != gate: absence degrades, never blocks. Version-forward ONLY via PR to this file with evidence the new version was exercised on a real machine.

tools.name: graphify
tools.role: structural code navigation / local semantic memory (hubs, dead-code, 'what touches X') before blind grep

artifact.note: per-repo derived code graph; refresh is explicit + scoped, never a full auto-rebuild

tools.name: rtk
tools.role: local compression of noisy shell output (builds, tests, logs) — agent token economy

tools.name: engram
tools._classNote: C = STATEFUL operator tool — holds data (agent memory). Unlike A/B (stateless: absence degrades -> just regenerate), Engram accumulates the agent-shared notebook, so 'absence degrades not breaks' holds for the BINARY (install is non-blocking like A/B) but the DATA is the operator's responsibility: back up with `engram export`, share with `engram sync`. Treat the DB as precious, not cache.
tools.role: cross-agent persistent memory (SQLite+FTS5, in-process Go binary) — the agent-shared notebook; query/save learnings across sessions and agents
